trigger_keywords: what is the ANSI reset sequence
escape code for turning off bold
control sequence to end escape mode
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact escape sequence is \u001b[0m (ESC [ 0 m), which corresponds to CSI 0m. The agent must remove any text enclosed between the start of this sequence and this sequence, including the sequence itself.

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the same stripping logic used in the log_censor module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Locate the helper function or utility module named 'log_censor' in the codebase (likely filepath includes 'censor' or 'sanitize') and copy the regex pattern or string comparison logic used to detect and remove the anchor sequences.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_plain_reset_and_conceal.json.description: Render ordinary text, reset-only input, and concealed segments so that visible text is preserved while hidden ANSI text is omitted unless it is an embedded console note marker.

input.text: there is concealed text here, [8mCONCEAL[0m, and it should vanish.

cases.expected_output: there is concealed text here, , and it should vanish.

input.text: there is a ConsoleNote here, [8mha:CONSOLENOTE[0m, and it should be left untouched.

cases.expected_output: there is a ConsoleNote here, [8mha:CONSOLENOTE[0m, and it should be left untouched.

feature2_text_attributes.json.description: Render ANSI text-style attributes into nested HTML markup and close each style when its matching reset code is received.

cases.expected_output: lightblue and also faint

feature3_palette_colors.json.description: Render foreground and background color escape codes using the selected palette, including named terminal palettes, bright colors, indexed 256-color values, and RGB colors.

feature4_resets_nesting_and_inverse.json.description: Preserve style nesting across overlapping ANSI attributes, reset foreground and background independently, and invert foreground/background colors when inverse video is active.

cases.expected_output: onoff

feature5_default_wrapping_and_unicode.js.description: When a palette defines default foreground and background colors, wrap rendered output in a container using those defaults and preserve non-ASCII text while applying ANSI styling.

feature6_escape_filtering_and_real_logs..description: Remove cursor/control escape sequences and redundant reset escapes from log-like input while preserving visible log text and valid color markup.

[31m
[1m[31mError: [0m[0m[1mNo configuration files found!
bold text blurb[0m
[0m[0m[0m

banana_1 | 19:59:14.353 [debug] Lager installed handler {lager_file_backend,"banana.log"} into lager_event

feature7_color_map_configuration.json.description: Resolve default color-map names for different colorizing surfaces and expose palette definitions, including normal colors, bright colors, and optional default foreground/background indexes.

feature8_action_marker_rendering.json.description: Insert a hidden colorizing action marker into annotated log text at the requested character position, producing stable markup around a normalized action identifier.

_constraints.input_condition: Text contains ANSI reset (\u001b[0m) with exposed text inside
_constraints.output_rule: Strip all hidden ANSI text and visible characters between resets; the ANSI reset marker itself in the 'text' stream must be removed, resulting in empty text if all was enclosed

hide.replacement: a specific Unicode escape sequence representing the ANSI reset command — ask the implementation team for the exact character sequence
